title: Crypto scams targeting Gen Z through WhatsApp and social media, using fake trading platforms to extract money via fake release fees.
ASIC warns of AI-powered investment scams proliferating on social media with deepfake celebrity endorsements and fake trading bots promising passive income.
ATO and ASIC warn of superannuation scams targeting Australians through social media and cold calls offering illegal early SMSF access.
Scammers impersonating ACCC and NASC by spoofing official phone numbers to request personal information and financial details from unsuspecting Australians.
Spike in Qantas phishing scams via email and SMS creating urgency to claim refunds or redeem points, using official branding and logos.
Australia Post phishing scams via SMS asking recipients to confirm delivery details after failed delivery, with fake tracking numbers embedded.
Australians targeted by voice-cloning and deepfake video scams, with AI-generated celebrity endorsements and fake family emergency calls circulating.
Family impersonation scams where fraudsters pose as relatives requesting urgent cash via WhatsApp or SMS, with voice cloning technology used.
National Anti-Scam Centre reports $2.18 billion lost to scams in 2025 across 481k reports, with investment and phishing scams dominating losses.
Betting and sports investment scams tripled losses to $2.4 million in 2025, with rising reports particularly among younger age groups and First Nations people.
Business invoice scams where criminals intercept emails and redirect payments to fraudulent accounts after altering supplier invoice details.
Identity theft scams targeting share portfolios, with criminals using stolen ID documents to create fraudulent trading accounts and sell victim shares.
